name: Hiroshima Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the deep red of historic prosperity and the rising sun
purity, peace and the bright future of Hiroshima

description: A deep-red field charged at the centre with a white emblem stylising the katakana ヒ of the prefecture's name.
history: Hiroshima's prefectural symbol was adopted on 1 July 1968 to mark the centenary of the Meiji restoration. The flag, with the white emblem on a maroon field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: a deep red recalling the historic dye and prosperity of the prefecture
a stylised katakana ヒ (hi) of 広島 (Hiroshima), enclosed in a circle representing harmony among the prefecture's people and the development of the region.
